source_issue.title: Document to run `make install` when changing a dependency

summary_markdown:
```markdown
## What this issue is about
This issue addresses the need for better documentation regarding the process of running `make install` when changing a dependency in the Volto project. Currently, new contributors may not be aware of this important step, which also involves committing the updated `pnpm-lock.yaml` file.

## What needs to be done
We need to document the `make install` step clearly in the Volto Contributing section to ensure that all contributors, especially newcomers, understand its importance when modifying dependencies. 

## Helpful background
The discussion suggests that existing documentation is lacking in this area, with only a couple of mentions of `make install`. Improving this will help maintain consistency in contributions and reduce confusion for new developers.

## Good first steps
1. Review the current documentation related to contributing, specifically the pages on [developing core](https://6.docs.plone.org/volto/contributing/developing-core.html) and [linting](https://6.docs.plone.org/volto/contributing/linting.html).
2. Suggest wording for a new section that includes the `make install` step and its significance, possibly integrating it into the *testing* documentation.
3. Coordinate with others involved in this discussion, like @Kathrina-dev and @wesleybl, to refine the documentation further.
```
